title: Privacy Policy

ui.heroDescription: TimePro stores your data on your device. If you enable iCloud Sync, supported records are also stored in your private iCloud database.

intro: This Privacy Policy explains how the TimePro mobile application (the "Application"), created by Thompson Costa Honorato (the "Service Provider"), stores and handles information. TimePro is a commercial service provided on an "AS IS" basis.

sections.title: What information does the Application handle?
content: The Application stores the information you enter or generate while using its features:

items: Preferences and configuration stored with UserDefaults, such as language, formatting, calculation, sound, haptic feedback, export, and synchronization choices.
Calculation history stored with Core Data, including expressions, results, intermediate steps, grouping information, and timestamps.

Work records stored with Core Data, including saved job names, hourly rates, currencies, default schedules and breaks, work dates and times, durations, payment status, payment records, archive status, and notes.
Birthday records stored with Core Data, including names, dates of birth, and notes.

content: Payment records are information you enter to organize work earnings and payments. TimePro does not request or store payment card numbers, bank account credentials, or other payment credentials.

sections.title: Local storage and optional iCloud Sync
content: TimePro stores your settings and records locally on your device so the Application can work without an account or a connection to a developer-operated server.

If iCloud Sync is available to you and you choose to enable it, the Core Data records described above are automatically uploaded to and downloaded from Apple's CloudKit service. They are stored in your private iCloud database and made available to devices signed in to the same Apple Account.
Preferences stored with UserDefaults are not part of TimePro's CloudKit synchronization.
If you disable iCloud Sync, you can choose either to keep the previously synchronized data in iCloud or to delete it from iCloud.

sections.title: How is information used?
content: The information is used only to provide Application functionality, including calculations, history, work and earnings records, birthday calculations, reports and exports, preference restoration, Premium entitlement management, and optional synchronization across your devices.

sections.title: Sharing and external services
content: The Service Provider does not operate a server that receives the content of your locally stored records, does not include third-party advertising or analytics services in TimePro, and does not sell your data, use it for advertising, or use it to track you across apps or websites.

When iCloud Sync is enabled, Apple processes synchronized records through CloudKit on your behalf. Apple services may also process the technical identifiers and synchronization metadata required to operate and protect the service. The Service Provider does not receive the contents of your private CloudKit database through a TimePro backend.

Premium purchases and restorations are processed by Apple through StoreKit. TimePro receives product, transaction, and entitlement information needed to unlock and restore Premium features, but does not receive your payment card or banking details.

Reports and export files are generated locally. When you use the system share sheet, the exported information is sent only to the application, person, or service you select. That destination's privacy practices apply after you share it.

When you voluntarily contact support, TimePro opens your email application with a draft that may include your device model, iOS version, Application version, locale or language settings, and the message you write. The email is sent only after you choose to send it and is processed by your email provider and the Service Provider's support mailbox.

sections.title: Device permissions and sensor data
content: The Application does not request precise or approximate location and does not access your system address book, photos, camera, microphone, health data, or calendar. People shown in TimePro's birthday feature are records that you create manually; they are not imported from the Contacts application.

sections.title: Retention and deletion
content: Local settings and records remain on your device until you delete them through the Application, erase the device, or uninstall the Application, subject to any device backups managed by you or Apple.

Uninstalling TimePro removes its local data from that device but does not automatically delete data that was previously synchronized to iCloud. If you reinstall TimePro and enable iCloud Sync again with the same Apple Account, that data may be downloaded again.

To delete synchronized data, use the "Disable and Delete Cloud Data" option in TimePro's iCloud Sync settings. This deletes TimePro's CloudKit data from your private iCloud database and removes the synchronized data from your other devices. You may also manage applicable iCloud data through the controls Apple provides.

Information that you voluntarily export, share, or send by email is retained according to the choices and policies of the recipients and services you selected. Copies outside TimePro are not deleted when you delete or uninstall the Application.

sections.title: Children
content: The Application is not directed to children under 13, and the Service Provider does not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe a child has sent personal information to the Service Provider through a support request, contact {{email}} so appropriate action can be taken. Where applicable, a parent or legal guardian must provide any consent required by local law.

sections.title: Security
content: Local data is protected by the security features of your device. When iCloud Sync is enabled, synchronized data is stored in your private CloudKit database and protected by Apple's iCloud security measures. No storage or transmission method is completely secure, so absolute security cannot be guaranteed.

sections.title: Changes to this policy
content: This Privacy Policy may be updated when the Application's features or data practices change. The effective date shown below will be updated when a revised policy is published. You should review this page periodically.

sections.title: Your choices and consent
content: iCloud Sync is optional. You can use TimePro with local storage only, disable synchronization while keeping cloud data, or disable synchronization and delete cloud data. By using the Application, you consent to the handling of information as described in this Privacy Policy.

sections.title: Contact us
content: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or TimePro's data practices, contact the Service Provider at {{email}}.

footer: This Privacy Policy is effective as of {{date}}.
